summary: The PR adds markdown coverage, but it also breaks both unchanged automation contracts the description says must stay intact: the no-flag CLI path no longer emits JSON, and explicit `--output json` now returns quoted markdown.

findings.title: Changing the CLI default to markdown breaks the nightly no-flag consumer
findings.impact: The nightly fanout still shells out to `release-readiness input.json` without `--output` and parses stdout as JSON keyed by the top-level `version` field. After this default flip, the unchanged consumer receives markdown instead of a machine-readable object, so the historical automation path described in the PR stops working.

findings.title: The `json` renderer now routes to markdown, so explicit `--output json` loses the object contract
findings.impact: Mapping `json` to `render_markdown` makes `export_report(..., output='json')` return a string. The unchanged CLI then `json.dumps` that string and emits a quoted markdown blob, so callers no longer receive the top-level `version` field and other dictionary keys that explicit JSON consumers rely on.
